Midjourney Review Calendar — AI helper guide
===========================================

Purpose
- Tracks daily Midjourney tasks (images, videos, publishing, extras) with autosave in the browser.
- Data lives in localStorage; JSON import/export keeps it portable for agents or backups.

Key entry points
- UI: index.html (loads style.css, app.js).
- Logic: app.js (MidjourneyCalendar class; storage keys, calendar render, task panel, import/export).
- Styles: style.css (2026-inspired theme, responsive grid, focus states).

How to run
1) cd /workspace/mj-calendar
2) Open index.html in a browser (double-click or use `python -m http.server 8000` then visit http://localhost:8000 ).
3) No API keys required. If you add one later, place it in .env (see .env.example) and keep it out of commits.

Useful commands (from repo root)
- python -m http.server 8000  # quick local server
- npx @axe-core/cli index.html  # accessibility audit (requires Node/npm)

Docs map
- README.md: overview + How to Get Started.
- ARCHITECTURE.md: storage model, UI flow, files.
- SETUP.md: running locally/hosting.
- USAGE.md: how to tick tasks, add tasks, import/export.
- TROUBLESHOOTING.md: quick fixes.
- PLAN.md: task history and status icons.
- CHANGELOG.md: dated version entries.
- COSTS.md: expected running/hosting/API costs and limits.
- ACCESSIBILITY.md: WCAG 2.2 AA targets and how to test.
- AGENTS.md: house rules for agents.

AI-readiness tips
- Use the JSON export schema (versioned payload with tasks + days) for MCP/skills.
- Avoid hard-coding secrets; prefer environment variables and document new keys in SETUP/README/COSTS.
- Keep UI changes accessible (focus states, aria labels, keyboard navigation).
